skill.name: react-flow-architect
skill.description: Guides developers in building advanced ReactFlow applications with hierarchical graphs, optimized rendering, Dagre layouts, search, focus modes, and graph state patterns.
skill.summary: Build production-ready ReactFlow graph interfaces with hierarchy, layout, and performance patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown code fences, TypeScript examples, template literals, and ordinary graph terminology. No executable shell commands, weak cryptographic algorithms, network calls, environment access, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration behavior were found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals inside ReactFlow examples. They are documentation syntax, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contexts show fenced TypeScript and JavaScript examples plus template string formatting. No shell interpreter, command API, or execution instruction appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Were Text Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations refer to words such as nodes, edges, Graph, maps, Sets, and layout examples. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, crypto library, hashing function, or credential handling was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review and keyword search found graph data structures and UI code only. The suspicious cryptographic algorithm names were not present in the skill file.

content.value_statement: Complex graph interfaces are hard to structure, optimize, and maintain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code reusable ReactFlow patterns for hierarchy, layout, search, state, and performance.
actual_capabilities: Designs ReactFlow node and edge schemas for hierarchical graph interfaces.
Provides expand and collapse patterns for tree-style navigation.
Shows memoization and incremental rendering techniques for large graphs.
Integrates Dagre-based automatic layout with cached and debounced calculations.
Outlines focus mode, search navigation, undo, redo, and graph state management.
Includes a graph analyzer concept for estimating rendering and complexity issues.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not ship executable React components.
Example snippets may need adaptation for the installed ReactFlow version.
The performance analyzer is illustrative and not a complete production profiler.
It does not cover backend graph storage, authentication, or deployment.

use_cases.title: Process Map Builder
use_cases.description: Create an interactive process graph with expandable stages, selected edges, and stable node rendering.

use_cases.title: Architecture Dependency Viewer
use_cases.description: Model service dependencies with auto-layout, focus mode, and search-driven navigation.

use_cases.title: Large Graph Optimization Review
use_cases.description: Audit an existing ReactFlow screen for excessive renders, slow layout work, and memory growth.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Graph
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a small ReactFlow view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-architect skill to create a basic ReactFlow graph with typed nodes, typed edges, and a simple custom node component.

prompt_templates.title: Add Hierarchical Navigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate tree behavior for parent and child nodes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-architect skill to add expand and collapse behavior for hierarchical nodes. Include visible node calculation and edge filtering.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Large Graph
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced performance work on a graph with many nodes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-architect skill to review my ReactFlow graph for render bottlenecks. Recommend memoization, incremental rendering, and layout caching changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Graph Editor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture for a full graph application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-flow-architect skill to design a production ReactFlow editor with Dagre layout, search navigation, focus mode, undo, redo, and persistence boundaries.

output_examples.input: Build a ReactFlow process map with expandable departments and tasks.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering node schema, parent-child traversal, visible edge filtering, expand state, and memoized custom nodes.

output_examples.input: My graph becomes slow after several hundred nodes.
output_examples.output: A performance review that prioritizes React.memo, stable callbacks, incremental rendering, debounced layout, and cache cleanup.

output_examples.input: Add search and focus mode to an architecture graph.
output_examples.output: A navigation design with query filtering, parent expansion, selected-node centering, connected-node isolation, and smooth visual transitions.

best_practices: Keep node data typed and separate graph state from view-only UI state.
Memoize custom nodes, derived edges, and layout results before scaling graph size.
Debounce expensive layout work and clean caches when graph data changes.
anti_patterns: Rebuilding every node and edge for simple expand or selection changes.
Running automatic layout on every render or pointer movement.
Storing large graph objects in state when refs or derived maps are enough.

faq.question: Does this skill create a complete ReactFlow app?
faq.answer: It provides patterns, snippets, and architecture guidance. A developer still integrates them into the target application.

faq.question: Can it help with very large graphs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on incremental rendering, memoization, caching, and reducing visible nodes for large datasets.

faq.question: Does it require Dagre?
faq.answer: No. Dagre is shown as one layout option. The state and rendering patterns can work with other layout engines.

faq.question: Is it limited to TypeScript?
faq.answer: Most examples use TypeScript, but the architecture can be adapted to JavaScript React projects.

faq.question: Does it include backend storage patterns?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on frontend graph rendering, interaction, layout, and state management.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it during implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill gives Claude Code and Codex concrete patterns to apply while editing ReactFlow code.
